title: Transporting Valuable Fine Art: How We Protect Canvas Paintings and Wooden Sculptures from Dusty Corridors
excerpt: Fine art transport Addis: protect canvas paintings wooden sculptures dusty corridors, climate wrap, custom crating, gallery quality moving Ethiopia.
content:
Transporting valuable fine art: how we protect canvas paintings and wooden sculptures from dusty corridors

Addis corridors coat art with fine dust—canvas weave traps particles, wooden sculpture grain ingrains grit if unsealed truck ride. Fine art transport demands sealed crating, vertical canvas discipline, and climate awareness not blanket over corner propped in open truck bed.

This guide protects canvas paintings and wooden sculptures during valuable art transport.

Canvas painting protocol

  • Corner protectors cardboard.
  • Glassine paper face no bubble direct pressure.
  • Vertical travel picture box or mirror crate.
  • Climate bag Kremt humidity swing.
  • No fingerprint oil edge—cotton gloves.

Wooden sculpture protocol

Grain orientation note; protrusions foam carve; wrap breathable fabric not plastic sweat; crate immobilize zero rattle test; insect inspection export if international.

Dust corridor tactics

Sealed truck not open bed; cab transport museum piece tier optional; unload direct interior no courtyard wait; HEPA vacuum frame grooves destination before hang.

Insurance documentation

Appraisal; condition report both ends; declared value maximum; photographer witness optional high tier.

Active Movers fine art tier

Custom crate shop partner; vertical load mandate; dust-sealed truck policy.

Conclusion

Fine art survives dusty Addis through sealed vertical crating, breathable sculpture wrap, enclosed transport—canvases and wood arrive exhibition-ready not restoration emergency.
author: Active Movers Team
